label: Lower back contusion without spine or neurologic red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit an uncomplicated lower back contusion.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a lower back contusion. We did not find spine fracture concern, neurologic deficit, cauda equina symptoms, infection concern, or another back emergency today.
home: Rest the lower back from activity that makes pain worse.
Use ice, heat, gentle motion, support, or activity limits only as your clinician recommended.
Return to normal activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Do not use medicine to push through worsening pain, numbness, or weakness.
return: New leg weakness, numbness, saddle numbness, trouble urinating, loss of bowel control, or trouble walking.
New deformity, severe worsening pain, inability to use the area, or a new injury.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, drainage, or symptoms not improving as expected.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, spine clinic, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated lower back contusion.
No spine fracture concern, new neurologic deficit, cauda equina concern, spinal infection concern, severe pain, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed spine plan.
exclusion: Spine fracture or major trauma concern, cauda equina symptoms, new neurologic deficit, spinal infection concern, severe or progressive pain, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed spine plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports bruise/contusion care and conservative low-back red-flag escalation. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated lower back contusion with spine fracture, neurologic, cauda equina, and infection red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of uncomplicated lower back contusion
no neurologic deficit or cauda equina symptoms

home: Rest the sore area from activity that makes pain worse.
